director.name: 尼亚兹·穆斯塔法 Niazi Mostafa

introduction: Another classic comedy by Egypt's answer to Lou Costello, the Egyptian "King of Comedy" Ismail Yassin. The rubber faced comedian is joined here by some of the best known comedic actors of Egyptian cinema, including Hassan Fayek and Abdel Salam Al Nabulsy. The story, by Egyptian comedy mainstay Abu Seoud El-Ibiary, is about a millionaire's son who gets lost in the jungle and is subsequently raised by "Cheetah" the gorilla, but the real fun begins when he is found and returned to civilization and high society. Among his antics are eating practically anything in sight, include a phonograph record and a "cheescake" magazine. Directed by Niazi Mostafa, whose 42 year career and 77 directing credits include titles such as Women of Pleasure and The Taxi of Love.
